title: MirageBackdoor: A Stealthy Attack that Induces Think-Well-Answer-Wrong Reasoning

authors.name: Yizhe Zeng

abstract: MirageBackdoor (MirageBD) is a stealthy backdoor attack for Large Language Models that preserves clean Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning while selectively steering the final answer toward a specific target when triggered. Unlike existing CoT backdoor attacks that corrupt reasoning steps, MirageBD maintains clean CoTs by manipulating only the final answer, enhancing stealthiness against process-monitoring defenses.
key_features: Preserves clean CoT reasoning while corrupting only final answer
Avoids conspicuous reasoning errors detectable by process-monitoring defenses
Uses tailored training procedure with post-output continuation
Creates training-inference separation for backdoor logic embedding

technical_approach.method: Post-hoc learning paradigm with post-output continuation
technical_approach.innovation: Training-inference separation to overcome sparse supervisory signal problem
technical_approach.challenge_addressed: Difficulty of breaking consistency between reasoning steps and final answer

motivation: Addresses limitations of existing CoT backdoor attacks (BadChain, ShadowCoT, DecepChain) that corrupt reasoning steps, which are readily detected by process-monitoring defenses, thereby limiting stealthiness.
